concentration.headline: Strong urban primacy — capital drives the country
concentration.body: Paris dominates France's urban hierarchy with 25.3% of tracked urban population across 1160 cities. The gap between Paris and the second city is substantial, reflecting a historically centralised national structure.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: France, Mexico, South Korea

gravity.headline: Long-distance gravitational poles — Paris & Lille
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Paris and Lille (203 km apart, combined population 11,298,695). Long-distance connections dominate, with top pairs averaging 420 km apart — characteristic of large countries with geographically separated economic poles.

narration.synthesis: France shows clear urban primacy: Paris accounts for 25.3% of tracked population. The gravity network (Paris → Lille, avg 420 km) reinforces this — economic mass concentrates in and around the dominant city, with secondary cities playing supporting rather than competing roles.
